source: Flocabulary G4 + Wordly Wise Book 4 + State Standards

words.definition: to successfully reach a goal through effort
words.example: She worked hard to achieve a perfect score on her test.

words.definition: the energy you use to do something
words.example: It takes a lot of effort to learn a new language.

words.definition: to try to do something, especially something difficult
words.example: He attempted to climb the rock wall on his first visit.

words.definition: to make something better than it was before
words.example: Practice every day to improve your handwriting.

words.definition: to grow or change over time into something bigger or better
words.example: Scientists develop new medicines to help sick people.

words.definition: forward movement toward a goal
words.example: She made great progress in reading this semester.

words.definition: to decide something firmly after thinking about it
words.example: The coach will determine who plays in the next game.

words.definition: to give all your attention to one thing
words.example: Focus on the teacher when she is explaining the lesson.

words.definition: to do well or get the result you wanted
words.example: If you keep trying, you will succeed.

words.definition: something difficult that tests your abilities
words.example: The math competition was a big challenge for our team.

words.definition: to look at how two or more things are alike or different
words.example: Compare the two stories and find what they have in common.

words.definition: to tell what something is like using words
words.example: Describe your favorite animal in three sentences.

words.definition: to look at something very carefully to learn more about it
words.example: The doctor will examine your eyes during the checkup.

words.definition: to talk about a topic with other people
words.example: Let's discuss our ideas for the group project.

words.definition: to make something clear so others can understand it
words.example: Can you explain how this machine works?

words.definition: to reach a decision or opinion after thinking about it
words.example: After reading the clues, I concluded the butler did it.

words.definition: to know what something is because you have seen it before
words.example: I can recognize her voice on the phone.

words.definition: what someone thinks or believes about something
words.example: In my opinion, chocolate ice cream is the best flavor.

words.definition: the cause or explanation for why something happens
words.example: The reason I was late was because I missed the bus.

words.definition: to help or hold something up; to back up with evidence
words.example: Support your answer with facts from the text.

words.definition: a test done to learn something new or prove an idea
words.example: We did an experiment to see which plant grows fastest.

words.definition: to watch something carefully to learn from it
words.example: Observe the caterpillar as it changes into a butterfly.

words.definition: the power needed to make things move, grow, or work
words.example: The sun gives us light and energy.

words.definition: a series of events that repeat in the same order
words.example: The water cycle includes evaporation, condensation, and rain.

words.definition: a series of steps that happen in order to make something
words.example: Baking a cake is a step-by-step process.

words.definition: the substance that something is made from
words.example: Wood is a natural material used to build houses.

words.definition: something useful that helps you do or make something
words.example: Water is an important natural resource.

words.definition: the natural world around us, including air, water, and land
words.example: We should protect the environment by recycling.

words.definition: a change caused by something; a result
words.example: Pollution has a bad effect on the air we breathe.

words.definition: facts or information that show whether something is true
words.example: The footprints were evidence that someone had been there.

words.definition: to work together with others to get something done
words.example: The students cooperated to clean up the classroom.

words.definition: to give something helpful, like time, ideas, or money
words.example: Each student contributed one idea to the class project.

words.definition: being the one who should take care of something
words.example: You are responsible for feeding the class hamster this week.

words.definition: a person who belongs to a city or country
words.example: Every citizen has the right to vote when they grow up.

words.definition: a feeling of caring about someone's feelings and rights
words.example: Show respect to your classmates by listening when they speak.

words.definition: a disagreement or fight between people or groups
words.example: The two friends had a conflict about which game to play.

words.definition: to find a solution to a problem or disagreement
words.example: They resolved their argument by taking turns.

words.definition: to join in and be part of an activity
words.example: Everyone should participate in the class discussion.

words.definition: a person who helps without being paid
words.example: My mom is a volunteer at the community garden.

words.definition: treating everyone equally and by the rules
words.example: The teacher made sure the test was fair for everyone.

words.definition: belonging to a time long ago in the past
words.example: Ancient Egyptians built the pyramids thousands of years ago.

words.definition: a period of one hundred years
words.example: This church is over a century old.

words.definition: relating to the present time; using the newest ideas
words.example: Modern technology makes communication much faster.

words.definition: a length of time in history or in daily life
words.example: The dinosaurs lived during the Jurassic period.

words.definition: a custom or belief passed down from one generation to the next
words.example: Eating turkey on Thanksgiving is an American tradition.

words.definition: the beliefs, customs, and way of life of a group of people
words.example: Korean culture values hard work and respect for elders.

words.definition: something that happens, especially something important
words.example: The school talent show was the biggest event of the year.

words.definition: to have an effect on how someone thinks or acts
words.example: Good books can influence the way you see the world.

words.definition: to start or create something that is meant to last
words.example: The settlers established a new town near the river.

words.definition: happening before the current one; earlier
words.example: In the previous chapter, the hero found the secret map.

words.definition: a person who writes books, stories, or articles
words.example: The author of this book also wrote three other novels.

words.definition: a person or animal in a story, movie, or play
words.example: The main character in the story is a brave young girl.

words.definition: a small piece of information about something
words.example: Add more details to your story to make it interesting.

words.definition: stories and books about things that are not real
words.example: Harry Potter is a popular work of fiction.

words.definition: to tell someone facts or important information
words.example: The news report will inform people about the storm.

words.definition: the reason why something is done or made
words.example: The purpose of this exercise is to build your vocabulary.

words.definition: to tell the main points of something in a short way
words.example: Summarize the story in three sentences.

words.definition: the main message or lesson in a story
words.example: The theme of the book is that kindness always wins.

words.definition: a short section of writing from a book or article
words.example: Read the passage carefully and answer the questions.

words.definition: a story or account of events told in order
words.example: She wrote a personal narrative about her summer vacation.

words.definition: to figure out a number or amount using math
words.example: Calculate the total cost of three books at five dollars each.

words.definition: the same in number, size, or amount
words.example: Both teams have an equal number of players.

words.definition: to make a close guess about a number or amount
words.example: Can you estimate how many jelly beans are in the jar?

words.definition: how far apart two places or things are
words.example: The distance from our house to school is two miles.

words.definition: a number or a shape drawn with lines
words.example: Write the figure for one thousand: 1,000.

words.definition: how much something is worth in money or importance
words.example: The value of this coin is fifty cents.

words.definition: something that repeats in a regular way
words.example: The wallpaper has a pattern of blue and white stripes.

words.definition: the whole amount when everything is added together
words.example: The total of 5 plus 7 is 12.

words.definition: to split something into equal parts or groups
words.example: Divide the pizza into eight slices so everyone gets one.

words.definition: correct and without any mistakes
words.example: Make sure your measurements are accurate before cutting.

words.definition: a large area of land that has something in common
words.example: The desert region gets very little rain each year.

words.definition: the line that separates two countries or areas
words.example: The river forms the border between the two states.

words.definition: the land along the edge of the ocean
words.example: We drove along the coast and saw beautiful beaches.

words.definition: the total number of people living in a place
words.example: The population of our city is about 500,000 people.

words.definition: the usual weather in a place over many years
words.example: The tropical climate is warm and rainy all year.

words.definition: relating to the countryside, not the city
words.example: They live in a rural area surrounded by farmland.

words.definition: relating to a city or town
words.example: Urban areas have more buildings and traffic than rural areas.

words.definition: the exact place where something is
words.example: Can you find the location of our school on this map?

words.definition: the natural features of an area of land that you can see
words.example: The mountain landscape was covered with snow.

words.definition: a way or path from one place to another
words.example: We took the shortest route to get to the park.

words.definition: to show your feelings or thoughts through words or actions
words.example: She used a painting to express her happiness.

words.definition: to answer or react to something
words.example: Please respond to the question by raising your hand.

words.definition: to share ideas or information with someone
words.example: We communicate with our pen pals by writing letters.

words.definition: to make someone agree with you or believe something
words.example: She convinced her parents to let her get a puppy.

words.definition: to give reasons for or against an idea; to disagree
words.example: The two sides argued about the best solution.

words.definition: to make something known to many people
words.example: The principal announced the winners of the contest.

words.definition: to get someone to do something by giving good reasons
words.example: I tried to persuade my brother to share his cookies.

words.definition: to change words from one language to another
words.example: Can you translate this sentence from Spanish to English?

words.definition: to make a book, article, or writing available for people to read
words.example: The school will publish the best student essays online.

words.definition: to stand for or speak on behalf of someone or something
words.example: The star on the flag represents the state of Texas.

words.definition: to make something new that did not exist before
words.example: The artist used recycled materials to create a sculpture.

words.definition: to plan and draw how something will look or work
words.example: The students designed a robot for the science fair.

words.definition: to change something completely into something different
words.example: Rain can transform a dry field into a green meadow.

words.definition: to join or link two or more things together
words.example: Connect the dots to see the hidden picture.

words.definition: to choose something carefully from a group
words.example: Select the best answer for each question.

words.definition: to gather things together into one place
words.example: We collect cans for the recycling program.

words.definition: to mix or join two or more things together
words.example: Combine the flour and eggs to make the batter.

words.definition: to put things in a certain order or position
words.example: Arrange the books on the shelf from A to Z.

words.definition: to put something new in the place of something old
words.example: We need to replace the broken window before winter.

words.definition: to produce or create something, especially energy or ideas
words.example: Wind turbines generate electricity without pollution.

words.definition: the ability to do something even when you are scared
words.example: It takes courage to stand up for what is right.

words.definition: willing to share and give more than is expected
words.example: The generous family donated food to the shelter.

words.definition: able to wait calmly without getting angry or upset
words.example: Be patient — the cookies will be done in ten minutes.

words.definition: always telling the truth and not cheating
words.example: An honest person admits when they make a mistake.

words.definition: feeling sure about yourself and your abilities
words.example: She felt confident before her piano performance.

words.definition: feeling worried or nervous about something
words.example: He felt anxious before his first day at a new school.

words.definition: feeling thankful for something good
words.example: I am grateful for my wonderful family.

words.definition: feeling upset because you cannot do something
words.example: She felt frustrated when the puzzle was too hard.

words.definition: wanting to learn or know more about something
words.example: The curious boy asked a hundred questions about space.

words.definition: having a strong decision to do something and not give up
words.example: The determined girl practiced until she could do a cartwheel.

words.definition: to be easy to understand; to be logical
words.example: Read it again — does it make sense now?

words.definition: to happen or occur
words.example: The concert will take place on Saturday night.

words.definition: to complete a task or put a plan into action
words.example: The team carried out the experiment step by step.

words.definition: to learn or discover new information
words.example: I want to find out who won the science fair.

words.definition: to rely on someone or something for help
words.example: Young birds depend on their parents for food.

words.definition: to stop trying or quit something
words.example: Don't give up — you are almost at the finish line!

words.definition: to prepare or arrange something for use
words.example: We set up the tent before it got dark.

words.definition: to separate into smaller parts; to stop working
words.example: Let's break down the problem into smaller steps.

words.definition: to search for information in a book or online
words.example: Look up the definition of any word you don't know.

words.definition: to find something by chance
words.example: I came across an old photo while cleaning my room.

words.definition: used to show a different point of view
words.example: I like summer. On the other hand, winter has snow days.

words.definition: because of something that happened before
words.example: She studied hard, and as a result, she passed the test.

words.definition: also; used to add more information
words.example: We need pencils. In addition, we need erasers.

words.definition: used to give an example
words.example: Many animals hibernate — for instance, bears sleep all winter.

words.definition: based on what someone or something says
words.example: According to the weather report, it will rain tomorrow.

words.definition: the way someone thinks about or sees something
words.example: The story is told from the dog's point of view.

words.definition: to create something; to become friends again after a fight
words.example: The children made up a new game during recess.

words.definition: to represent or mean something
words.example: The letters U.S.A. stand for United States of America.

words.definition: no less than a certain amount; if nothing else
words.example: You should read at least two books this month.

words.definition: until now; up to this point
words.example: We've learned 100 new words so far this year.
